nagpuri.food: Green fodder, dry straw, concentrates, mineral supplements
nagpuri.care: Regular bathing, deworming every 3 months, hoof trimming
nagpuri.description: Nagpuri buffalo is a draught-cum-dairy breed from central India, known for its white coat with black patches and curved horns. Milk fat content is around 7-8%.

nili-ravi.food: High-quality green fodder, silage, grain-based concentrates
nili-ravi.care: Wallowing facility needed, regular veterinary checkups, adequate water supply
nili-ravi.description: Nili-Ravi is one of the highest milk-producing buffalo breeds in the world, yielding 1800-2500 litres per lactation. Recognized by its characteristic 'wall eye' (blue eye) feature.

pandharpuri.origin: Solapur and Kolhapur districts, Maharashtra, India
pandharpuri.food: Green fodder, dry roughage, oilseed cakes, mineral mixtures
pandharpuri.care: Regular grooming, shade during summer, vaccination schedule
pandharpuri.description: Pandharpuri buffalo is known for its distinctive long, curved sword-shaped horns. It is a dual-purpose breed valued for both milk (6-8% fat) and draught work in Maharashtra.

surti.origin: Kaira and Baroda districts, Gujarat, India
surti.food: Green grass, jowar stover, groundnut cake, balanced mineral feed
surti.care: Regular milking schedule, clean housing, seasonal deworming
surti.description: Surti buffalo is a medium-sized dairy breed from Gujarat, yielding around 1200-1500 litres per lactation with high fat content of 8-9%. It is well-adapted to hot and humid climates.

toda.food: Natural highland grasses, shrubs, supplementary mineral licks
toda.care: Minimal intervention needed; semi-extensive management system
toda.description: Toda buffalo is a rare and indigenous breed reared by the Toda tribe of the Nilgiri Hills. It is considered sacred in their culture and is managed under a semi-wild free-grazing system. Milk yield is low but very rich in fat.
